title: ADHD - Bryan Schneider - Christ Centered Sermon Prep

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="ADHD Expository Workflow - Based on Christ Centered Preaching. This workflow is designed to finish a sermon in roughly 14 hours of work."/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Pray - Set timer for 15 min

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="Extremely Important! Pray and ask God for help. Especially with your easily distracted mind. "/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Translate the Text - Set timer for 2 hours

respond.prompt: What would your own translation be?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="What would your own translation be?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Were there any really odd words?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Were there any really odd words?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Was there repetition?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Was there repetition? "/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Were there any references to other texts?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Were there any references to other texts?"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Digest - Set time for 1 hour

respond.prompt: Write out a prayer for each phrase, sentence, or paragraph. Make this a time for spiritual worship.
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Write out a prayer for each phrase, sentence, or paragraph. Make this a time for spiritual worship. "/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Exegesis - Set timer for 1 hour

respond.prompt: Are there any significant textual variants?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Are there any significant textual variants? "/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Is there any figurative language?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Is there any figurative language?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: What literary typing is there?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="What literary typing is there?"/></Paragraph>

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="It is helpful to follow the sentence digram to see what the grammar says the subject and main verb is. This is especially important in epistles. "/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: What is the subject grammatically?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="What is the subject grammatically?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt:
What’s going on in this section of the book?
               Who is __________?
              What is the reader expected to know about them?
              What does ___________ mean in this context?
              Why is this detain included at this point?
              What purpose does _________ serve in the narrative?
              Why did the character say or do _____________?
              Was this expected/acceptable behavior at the time?
              What might be the original audience’s visceral reaction to this?
		how does it fit with ____________ [another part of the CUT, book, or Bible]?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="What"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="’"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="s going on in this section of the book?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="               Who is __________?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="              What is the reader expected to know about them?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="              What does ___________ mean in this context?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="              Why is this detain included at this point?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="              What purpose does _________ serve in the narrative?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="              Why did the character say or do _____________?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="              Was this expected/acceptable behavior at the time?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="              What might be the original audience"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="’"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="s visceral reaction to this?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="		how does it fit with ____________ [another part of the CUT, book, or Bible]?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: What grammatical constructions are being used? Are they significant?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="What grammatical constructions are being used? Are they significant?"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Get Quick Answers - Set timer for 30 min

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="Study Bibles can get you on the right track quickly. You are preaching and not writing an entire in depth exegesis paper. You only have a limited number of hours to get this task accomplished. "/></Paragraph><Paragraph/><Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="Remember time is of the essence. "/></Paragraph><Paragraph FontSize="11"/><Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="Don’t chase every rabbit just because it interests you. "/></Paragraph><Paragraph FontSize="11"/><Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="You only budget 30 minute for this. Start a timer. "/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt:
Open the custom guide - “Study Bible Collection” 
Is there anything significant you were missing?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Open the custom guide - “Study Bible Collection” "/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run Text="Is there anything significant you were missing?"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Context Questions - Set timer for 15 min

respond.prompt: What is the temporal setting?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="What is the temporal setting?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: What is the geographic setting?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="What is the geographic setting?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Are there cultural things that color this? Get the picture in your mind. This is not a rabbit trail but trying to get the lived experience of the passage.
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Are there cultural things that color this? Get the picture in your mind. This is not a rabbit trail but trying to get the lived experience of the passage."/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Big Picture - Set timer for 30 min

respond.prompt: What is the point of this passage in larger picture of this book?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="What is the point of this passage in larger picture of this book?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Are there surrounding units of thought that should be kept in mind?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Are there surrounding units of thought that should be kept in mind?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Where does this story fit in within the canonical and historical redemptive context?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Where does this story fit in within the canonical and historical redemptive context?"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Identify Christ - Set timer for 30 Min

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="This is especially important in OT passages. However, even in epistles Jesus must be preached even in the midst of imperative sections like the end of Ephesians. "/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt:
Historical Redemptive progress
What is the bottom level - personal history?
What is the middle level - national history?
What is the top level - receptive history?
What does this passage reveal about God and His will?
What does this passage mean in the context of the whole Bible?
What does this passage mean in the light of Jesus Christ?
What does the passage reveal about Jesus Christ?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="Historical Redemptive progress"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="What is the bottom level - personal history?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="What is the middle level - national history?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="What is the top level - receptive history?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="What does this passage reveal about God and His will?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="What does this passage mean in the context of the whole Bible?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="What does this passage mean in the light of Jesus Christ?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="What does the passage reveal about Jesus Christ?"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List>

respond.prompt:
promise-fulfillment
              Is there a messianic hope?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="promise-fulfillment"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="              "/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Is there a messianic hope?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt:
Typology
What is the historical: person, action, event, or institution?
Is the central message concerning God’s activity to redeem His people?
Does the type have symbolic meaning? If it does not - then it is not a type
Is the type used by God in His dealings with Israel?
Is there a significant analogy with its antitype
Is there an escalation from type to antitype?
What are the points of contrast between OT type and NT antitype?
What is the meaning of the symbol that escalates to Christ? (Greidanus 259)
Preach Christ - not the significance of the type but Christ himself
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="Typology"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="What is the"/><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text=" "/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="historical: person, action, event, or institution?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="Is the central message concerning God"/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="’"/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="s activity to redeem His people?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="Does the type have symbolic meaning? If it does not - then it is not a type"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="Is the type used by God in His dealings with Israel?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="Is there a significant analogy with its antitype"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="Is there an escalation from type to antitype?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="What are the points of contrast between OT type and NT antitype?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="What is the meaning of the symbol that escalates to Christ? (Greidanus 259)"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="Preach Christ - not the significance of the type but Christ himself"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List><Paragraph/>

respond.prompt:
Analogy
Are there any parallel situation with the New Testament?
What was God doing for Israel and what IS God in Christ doing for the church?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="Analogy"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Are there any parallel situation with the New Testament?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="What was God doing for Israel and what IS God in Christ doing for the church?"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List>

respond.prompt:
Longitudinal themes
What truth about God and his saving work is disclosed in this passage?
How is this particular truth carried forward in the history of revelation?
How does it find fulfillment in Christ?
Are any of the highways to the person, work, and teaching of Christ present?
(KOG, providence, covenant, presence of God, love of God, grace of God, justice, redemption, law, sin & guilt offerings, God’s concern for the poor, mediator, the Day of the Lord, & etc.)
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="Longitudinal themes"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="What truth about God and his saving work is disclosed in this passage?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="How is this particular truth carried forward in the history of revelation?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="inherit" Text="How does it find fulfillment in Christ?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="Are any of the highways to the person, work, and teaching of Christ present?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run Text="(KOG, providence, covenant, presence of God, love of God, grace of God, justice, redemption, law, sin & guilt offerings, God"/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="’"/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="s concern for the poor, mediator, the Day of the Lord, & etc.)"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List>

respond.prompt:
Contrast
A change for the OT to the new in Christ
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="Contrast"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="A change for the OT to the new in Christ"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List>

respond.prompt:
NT Reference
Is this passage referenced in the New Testament, thus giving us a new hermeneutical lens to understand this passage?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="NT Reference"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Is this passage referenced in the New Testament, thus giving us a new hermeneutical lens to understand this passage?"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Systematic & Biblical Studies Issues - Set timer for 30 min

respond.prompt: Are there any systematic issues that need to be examined or explained?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Are there any systematic issues that need to be examined or explained?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Are there any Biblical theology issues that need to be examined or explained?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Are there any Biblical theology issues that need to be examined or explained?"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Don’t live in a historical vacuum - Set timer for 30 min

respond.prompt: Are there any significant writings from the church fathers that change how I have understood this passage?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Are there any significant writings from the church fathers that change how I have understood this passage? "/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Smarter people than you have been here - Confessions - Set timer for 15 min

respond.prompt: Are there any confessional documents that reference this passage?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Are there any confessional documents that reference this passage?"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Questions still left? - Set timer for 30 min

respond.prompt:
Okay now you can turn to commentaries. Don’t get bogged down in this too quickly. 

You know the temptation to spend all week reading commentaries and waiting until Sunday morning to write your sermon. Don’t do that. 

Just find what you need. You haven’t even started writing the sermon yet.
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Okay now you can turn to commentaries. Don’t get bogged down in this too quickly. "/></Paragraph><Paragraph/><Paragraph><Run Text="You know the temptation to spend all week reading commentaries and waiting until Sunday morning to write your sermon. Don’t do that. "/></Paragraph><Paragraph/><Paragraph><Run Text="Just find what you need. You haven’t even started writing the sermon yet."/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Big Picture - Sermon Work
Determine the Subject - Set timer for 15 min

respond.prompt:
What question is this CUT (complete unit of thought - not a single word) designed to answer about God - his nature or covenant keeping - to ancient Israel?
What is the 1 thing this passage is about?
What is the unifying center?
What aspect of God’s character is being pointed out?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="What question is this CUT (complete unit of thought - not a single word) designed to answer about God - his nature or covenant keeping - to ancient Israel?"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="What is the 1 thing this passage is about?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="What is the unifying center?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="What aspect of God"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="’"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="s character is being pointed out?"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List>

steps.title: Develop the Theme - compliment - Set timer for 15 min

respond.prompt:
what is this passage saying about the subject?
What’s this writer saying about what he’s talking about? If the subject is a question, then the complement is the answer to that question, and the two together become the idea.
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="what is this passage saying about the subject?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="What"/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="’"/><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="s this writer saying about what he"/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="’"/><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="s talking about?"/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text=" If the subject is a question, then the complement is the answer to that question, and the two together become the idea."/></Paragraph><Paragraph/>

steps.title: Set forth Exegetical Point (EP) or {Original Theological Message - OTM} - Set timer for 15 min

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontItalic="True" Text="To truly preach expository sermons the original meaning and intent of the author must be preserved."/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt:
What was God saying through his Spirit through the author to the recipients of that time?
What is this text about? (subject)
What does the text say about it? (compliment)
What theme holds the entire message together?
Is the proposed EP a simple or complex sentence? (keep narrowing until simple)
Is the EP a propositional sentence?
Does the EP relate every sentence to the theme?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="What was God saying through his Spirit through the author to the recipients of that time?"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="What is this text about? (subject)"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="What does the text say about it? (compliment)"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="What theme holds the entire message together?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Is the proposed EP a simple or complex sentence? (keep narrowing until simple)"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Is the EP a propositional sentence?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Does the EP relate every sentence to the theme?"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List>

steps.title: Establish a Transition Sentence - Set timer for 15 min

respond.prompt: What in the text supports the EP - example: In what ways is gospel being advanced?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="What in the text supports the EP - example: In what ways is gospel being advanced?"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Develop Exegetical Outline - Set timer for 15 min

respond.prompt:
Outline the Text Exegetically (what is the internal logical flow)?
Identify textual support for EP
ask: what is being said about the EP?
does every point support the EP?
how does this point progress the argument of the EP?
how does this point relate to the point before and after it?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Outline the Text Exegetically (what is the internal logical flow)?"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Identify textual support for EP"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="ask: what is being said about the EP?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="does every point support the EP?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="how does this point progress the argument of the EP?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="how does this point relate to the point before and after it?"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List>

steps.title: ID the Fallen Condition Focus  - Set timer for 15 min

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontItalic="True" Text="This begins the process of moving the exegetical study to a sermon. A lecture describes the text. A sermon moves it from this is what God said to them into the realm of, "This is God's direction for you today." Spend time wrestling here in prayer. Think about the people in the congregation you'll be preaching to. What at the core of their person and experience is shared between the original audience and your hearers?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: What does the text say?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="What does the text say?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: What spiritual concern(s) did the text address (in its context)?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="What spiritual concern(s) did the text address (in its context)?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: What spiritual concerns do listeners share in common with those to (or about) whom the text what written?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="What spiritual concerns do listeners share in common with those to (or about) whom the text what written?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: What is the depravity factor? What in humanity rebels agains the text’s vision of God?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="What is the depravity factor? What in humanity rebels agains the text’s vision of God?"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Set forth Homiletical Point (HP) or {Take Home Truth - THT} - Set timer for 15 min

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontItalic="True" Text="A professor lectures. A pastor heralds God's word to His people today. The people of God should walk away with a sense that God's word is for them not just people who died 2000 years ago."/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Pray that God would give you a love for his people, insight into the word, and wisdom in explaining and applying God's word to His people's lives.
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Pray that God would give you a love for his people, insight into the word, and wisdom in explaining and applying God's word to His people's lives."/></Paragraph>

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="Did you actually pray? No, don’t skip this part. Actually go up and pray for reverence, wisdom, and love. Imagine the people in the pews. See their faces. "/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: What is God though me saying to his people today? What is the burden of the text? This will be the take home truth.
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="What is God though me saying to his people today? What is the burden of the text? This will be the take home truth."/></Paragraph>

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Now that you have your HP/THT - ask these questions:"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Is that based on the EP?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Is that based on the EP?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Is it written in the 2nd person or to an anonymous "someone"?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Is it written in the 2nd person or to an anonymous "someone"?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt:
Is it sharp? Will it stick? Does it get under the skin?
Should be drawing interest into the sermon
HP must be concise and memorable - Will people remember the point on Wednesday?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Is it sharp? Will it stick? Does it get under the skin?"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Should be drawing interest into the sermon"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="HP must be concise and memorable - Will people remember the point on Wednesday?"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List>

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="Now we’ll final edit the THT"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt:
HP - must have an eye toward the Fallen Condition Focus (FCF)
      []Take time specific language of EP/OTM and replace with contemporary equivalent
Here is one example from a book on moving an Exegetical Point (Original Theological Message) of a narrative passage to a Take Home Truth (Homiletical Point). Remember, our calling is to preach Christ and Him crucified. A christless sermon can live in a synagogue but ought to never among the blood bought sheep of Jesus. 

OTM: The Lord will use repentant kings of Israel to fulfill the Davidic Covenant, but despising the Lord’s word brings discipline.
THT: God will use us to fulfill his plan of salvation, but sin brings discipline.***
                       This should be amended to read Christologically
                       God used his King to fulfill the plan of salvation, and disciplined his son for us.***
Get it cleaned up more:
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="HP - must have an eye toward the Fallen Condition Focus (FCF)"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="      []"/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Take time specific language of EP/OTM and replace with contemporary equivalent"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="Here is one example from a book on moving an Exegetical Point (Original Theological Message) of a narrative passage to a Take Home Truth (Homiletical Point). Remember, our calling is to preach Christ and Him crucified. A christless sermon can live in a synagogue but ought to never among the blood bought sheep of Jesus. "/></Paragraph><Paragraph/><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="OTM: The Lord will use repentant kings of Israel to fulfill the Davidic Covenant, but despising the Lord"/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="’"/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="s word brings discipline."/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run Text="THT: God will use us to fulfill his plan of salvation, but sin brings discipline.***"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run Text="                      "/><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text=" This should be amended to read Christologically"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="                       God used his King to fulfill the plan of salvation, and disciplined his son for us.***"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Get it cleaned up more:"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Purpose Statement - Main Application - Set timer for 15 min

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="This is about the overall application of the sermon. There will be lots of smaller applications but what is the main imperative of the sermon? "/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt:
What should happen in the listeners as a result of hearing the sermon?
There is a divine imperative in preaching. Christian preaching extols Christ and sends Christians out to do.
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="What should happen in the listeners as a result of hearing the sermon?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="There is a divine imperative in preaching. Christian preaching extols Christ and sends Christians out to do. "/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: What did the writer expect to accomplish by putting this story here and telling it this way?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="What did the writer expect to accomplish by putting this story here and telling it this way?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt:
What do I expect to happen in the lives of the congregation after listening? (the 2 should point to the same goal)
Some applications are of faith (believe, receive, trust). Some applications are cognitive (understand, take to heart). Other times they are physical (go, flee, feed, wash). Others are spiritual (repent, hate, love, enjoy). 
Is there a measurable goal?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="What do I expect to happen in the lives of the congregation after listening? (the 2 should point to the same goal)"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontItalic="True" Text="Some applications are of faith (believe, receive, trust). Some applications are cognitive (understand, take to heart). Other times they are physical (go, flee, feed, wash). Others are spiritual (repent, hate, love, enjoy). "/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Is there a measurable goal?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: What does God require of the hearers of this passage?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="What does God require of the hearers of this passage?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Where does God Require it of me?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Where does God Require it of me?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Why must I do what God requires of me?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Why must I do what God requires of me?"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: How can I do what God requires of me?
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="How can I do what God requires of me?"/></Paragraph>

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="This is where the sermon writing process actually beings. "/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Formal Sermon Writing
Develop a broad preaching outline based on the exegetical outline - Set timer for 30 min

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontItalic="True" Text="Think of the preaching outline as a path you are wanting to take people. There is a main proposition and you are trying to bring people along on a path to get to the homiletical conclusion. Each point should solidify the truth of the HP or Take Home Truth."/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt: Type your outline here if you want
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Type your outline here if you want"/></Paragraph>

respond.prompt:
Each Point of the sermon can have each of these (but not necessarily all of them). Amplifying the points of your homiletical outline with become the body of your sermon.
Orientation
Explanation
Objections
Illustration
Application
respond.promptRichText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontBold="True" Text="Each Point of the sermon can have each of these (but not necessarily all of them). Amplifying the points of your homiletical outline with become the body of your sermon."/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Orientation"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Explanation"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Objections"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Illustration"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Application"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Amplify the main divisions - Set timer for 2 hours

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="Orientation:"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="People need to know where you are in the text, where you are in the sermon, where you are logically, where you are geographically, where you are in time. "/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontBold="True" Text="Explanation"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text=" - Prove psychologically & emotionally"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="            "/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="[]"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontBold="True" Text="List Questions"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="              "/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="What things wouldn"/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="’"/><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="t make sense to a modern audience?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run Text="                 Geography"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run Text="                 Customs"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run Text="                 Names"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run Text="                 History"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run Text="                 Theology"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run Text="                 Language"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run Text="                 Any other questions people might have from the text?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run Text="              Look at Scripture Index for reference to heads of doctrines"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontBold="True" Text="Objections & defense"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="validate the point against any emotional or intellectual doubts"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Answer the objection - Is this true? Can I buy this?"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List><Paragraph/><Paragraph><Run Text="Don’t be afraid to step into these areas. This is exactly where the Holy Spirit can work in the hardest of hearts. "/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontBold="True" Text="Illustrations"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Frame the picture"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="isolate the event & associate it with a concept"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="familiar emotions, identifiable dilemmas, personal traits, situational parallels "/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="see the cannons flash, feel the storm waves, fear the shoals - "/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontBold="True" Text="illustrations should be felt "/></Paragraph></ListItem></List></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Does it have the parts of a story?"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Intro, descriptive details, movement (actual or emotional), crisis, & conclusion"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List></ListItem></List><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="     "/><Run HasUnderline="True" FontItalic="True" FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontBold="True" Text="***Warning"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontItalic="True" Text=" "/><Run HasUnderline="True" FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontItalic="True" Text="Illustrations illuminate a truth - "/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run HasUnderline="True" FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontItalic="True" Text="Do NOT let the sermon be nothing but illustrations"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run HasUnderline="True" FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontItalic="True" Text="Slip into an illustration - shed the light - and get out - don"/><Run HasUnderline="True" FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontItalic="True" Text="’"/><Run HasUnderline="True" FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontItalic="True" Text="t get stuck in this mode"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontBold="True" Text="Application"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text=" - build around vision of God and depravity factor (FCF)"/></Paragraph><List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="What does this story teach me about God and his relationship with human beings?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="(needs, hurt, comfort, issues, trends, work/home/school)"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph Margin="0, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="so what?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph Margin="0, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="what will this truth look like when it is fleshed out in their lives?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph Margin="0, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="what is God calling His people to do through this story?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph Margin="0, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="don"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="’"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="t moralize - get at the why of the human heart"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List>
<Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="Application Prods"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Why It Matters"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="1. What if this truth was not real? What would the world be like? What would life be like?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="2. Why do you think people need this truth?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="3. How would you explain to someone that this truth is essential to finding true joy in this world?"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Dissecting the Message"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="1. Consider each word of the message. What does each word contribute? Why is each word important for what God is communicating to us here?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="2. What words or phrases would most significantly impact what this message is communicating if it were eliminated or altered?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="3. What are the three most important words in your stated message for this passage and why?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="4. If you had three minutes to tell this truth to a stranger, what part of it would you want to emphasize the most, and how might you do it, both in tone and explanation?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="5. What word or phrase could be easily glossed over (not given much attention), but is in fact quite significant to what's being communicated here?"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Clarifying the Message"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="1. How could someone misunderstand what this message is saying? What might help to clarify things for them?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="2. How could someone make this truth say too much or too little? What would it look like for someone to over-apply or under-apply this truth?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="3. What do you think people in general find most confusing about this truth? What do you think Christians specifically find most confusing about this truth?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="4. In what ways do you think this truth is misunderstood (e.g., overheard, underheard, poorly defined)?"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Analyzing Our Reaction"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="1. How do you find yourself responding to the message of this passage? What does our response teach us about ourselves and our own need for this passage?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="2. What do you find most encouraging about this passage's message? What do you find most challenging? And why?"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Addressing Our Struggle"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="1. Why do we fail to live in the good of the message of this passage?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="2. What do you think are the main reasons why people doubt or outright reject this message? What would be a helpful response to each challenge?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="3.When are the moments I need this message the most? "/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="4. What are some reasons why we find it functionally difficult to live as if this is true?"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Challenging Frameworks"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="1. How does the message of this passage challenge any operating frameworks we have?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="2. How does this truth challenge common cultural conceptions, viewpoints, and visions of reality? Does our culture and world tells us a different, competing message?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="3. How do you think someone living in a different culture might hear this message with a different nuance or focus than us? What might we learn from their perspective?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="4. What is one way you think God may want to adjust your thinking by revealing this truth to you?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="5. What false beliefs (about God, man, the world, etc.) can this truth combat?"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Shaping Loyalties"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="1. How might this truth strengthen your faith in God?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="2. How might this passage help you to persevere in, or alter, a goal you have in your life?"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Envisioning Its Realization"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="1. How might life be different this next week if trusted in this truth more?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="2. Envision a church characterized by this message? What would it look like?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="3. What sort of good fruit do you think this truth can stir up when a local church collectively enjoys this truth together?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="4. What is one risk you would take if you trusted in this truth more?"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Ministering Its Truth"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="1. How can you pray the truth of this passage for someone in the church?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="2. What is one way you could encourage someone with this truth?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="3. What might it look like to minister this truth to people in different situations (e.g., depressed, lonely, scared, overwhelmed, angry, hurt, arrogant, domineering, selfish, grumbling)?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="4. How might you pray this truth for this local church or for our city?"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="In Real Life"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="1. What are three real-life situations we can name where this truth could give our souls stability and hope?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="2. How do you think this truth can help you faithfully follow the Lord in particular situations this week?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="3. If someone watched your life and could hear all your thoughts, in what ways would they discover you not to be fully resting in and living out this truth?"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Pursuing Growth"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="1. What is something you can do to help you grow in trusting in this truth?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="2. What motivations does this passage present for fulfilling its applications? In light of this, what might it look like to pursue these applications?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="3. What part of this truth do you think you give the least amount of attention to? Why do you think that is? What might it look like to grow in thinking on that aspect and/or living in the good of it?"/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Reaching Unbelievers"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="1. How might you illustrate this truth to an unbeliever?"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="2. What questions do you think this message would raise for someone who has never heard the gospel before? "/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontBold="True" Text="Transition"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="0, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Try to think through how your homiletical points logically tie together. Transitions are often done well when they show how they "/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="1) Tie into the HP & FCF "/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="2) Summarize the previous point"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="3) Introduce the next point smoothly."/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Create introduction with HP/THT lead in - Set timer for 15 min

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run Text="Here are different types of Introductions"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1"><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" FontBold="True" Text="Front Porch"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="72, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="warm welcoming, come & talk"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="			Challenge / White Glove"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="72, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="impossible situation "/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="108, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="how can we evangelize - with/ hard statistic"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="challenge to prayer and fasting"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="2" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="I know this is going to be hard for you"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Mystery"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="veil assets of truth to unfold as sermon goes on"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="2" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Jesus was our priest -> unfold hows in points"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Examination"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="examined before the light of God’s word"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="2" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="before communion"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="2" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="prayer - point of examination is to fill holes"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="72, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="say it right up front that this is what we’re doing"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Paradox"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="God’s sovereignty and Evangelism"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="2" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Good paradoxes doesn’t confuse or go against each other but preaching pushes people with more confidence"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="God’s love and wrath"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Sola gratia and antinomianism"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Joseph - you meant it for evil but God meant it for good"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="2" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="“some of you are in horrible positions, we’ve been praying for you, weeping for you, how do we think about this?""/></Paragraph><Paragraph Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Occasion"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Holidays, culture, elections, national crisis, death, marriages, sacraments"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1"><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Quote"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Make sure it’s powerful and directed"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1"><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Question"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="you’re interested in what they’re talking about"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1"><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Short Story"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="children draw a picture of the story"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1"><Run FontFamily="Times" FontBold="True" Text="Ramrod"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="State HP"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="2" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="“Here’s what’s on my heart today""/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="1" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Just jump in, both feet"/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="2" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="“wake up""/></Paragraph><Paragraph Indent="2" Margin="36, 0, 0, 0"><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="“what’s really on my heart today is…”"/></Paragraph><Paragraph/><Paragraph><Run HasUnderline="True" FontFamily="Times" FontSize="14" FontBold="True" Text="Give Points of Sermon"/></Paragraph><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="MUST - have FCF"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Finish conclusion with repeat of THT/HP - Set timer for 15 min

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="Land the plane. Tell them wha they need to walk home with. Make it memorable."/></Paragraph>
<Paragraph><Run FontSize="11" Text="Use the sermon editor to help here - or type/write wherever"/></Paragraph>

steps.title: Finishing Questions to ask yourself - Set timer for 30 min

respond.prompt:
Have I been faithful to the text?
Have I prayed for the different age groups?
Am I hitting the head but missing the heart?
Have I appealed to emotions but neglected the intellect?
Is this a lecture or a sermon?
Are the people going to be spurred on toward loving God and His glory?
Have I read this missiologically?
Have I given God’s people tangible ways to apply His word this week?
Have I bound up the wounded?
Have I preached freedom to the captives?
Have I brought good news to the poor?
Does the intro have a fallen condition focus?
Have I landed the sermon - is it tied up so the people will remember what it was about?
respond.promptRichText: <List Kind="Disc"><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Have I been faithful to the text?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Have I prayed for the different age groups?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Am I hitting the head but missing the heart?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Times" Text="Have I appealed to emotions but neglected the intellect?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Is this a lecture or a sermon?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Are the people going to be spurred on toward loving God and His glory?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Have I read this missiologically?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Have I given God"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="’"/><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="s people tangible ways to apply His word this week?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Have I bound up the wounded?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Have I preached freedom to the captives?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Have I brought good news to the poor?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Does the intro have a fallen condition focus?"/></Paragraph></ListItem><ListItem><Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="Have I landed the sermon - is it tied up so the people will remember what it was about?"/></Paragraph></ListItem></List>

steps.title: No you’re not done. It’s too long and has too much junk. Don’t get lazy here. - Set timer for 45 min

parameters.richText: <Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="1) Pray over the sermon and plead with God. I pray for a few things each week: clarity, conviction, that it hits the heart, that it teaches the mind, that the sermon proclaims the glories Christ and Him crucified, that it will reform hearts, that it will convict of sin, and provoke God's people to good works. "/></Paragraph>

<Paragraph><Run FontFamily="Helvetica Neue" Text="2) Ruthlessly go through and edit it down. Not everything that can be said needs to be said. A clear sermon is better than a long sermon. Some of the best sermon writing is done at this point. Don't be afraid to use a red pen or the delete button."/></Paragraph>

author: Bryan Schneider
